entries.productionRisk: Unclassified validators can bypass severity policy and create theatre or petty hard-fails.
entries.proves: Every validation/build/smoke/env proof command is admitted to the matrix with severity and proof boundaries.
entries.doesNotProve: Product behavior, browser rendering, providers, or deployment.
entries.failureHandling: Update matrix first or remove unadmitted script from package.json.

entries.productionRisk: Missing source contracts cause implementation drift and false completion claims.
entries.proves: Required repo contracts and product guardrail concepts exist.
entries.doesNotProve: Runtime behavior or provider integrations.
entries.failureHandling: Restore missing contract or guarded concept.

entries.productionRisk: Env drift, client secret exposure, or missing deploy keys can break runtime/provider/deploy paths.
entries.proves: Registry/examples/vault envelope/gitignore safety and current Cloudflare scope are coherent.
entries.doesNotProve: Real credentials, provider validity, or Cloudflare dashboard state.
entries.failureHandling: Fix registry/examples/vault/gitignore. Do not hardcode keys.

entries.productionRisk: Plaintext secrets in source/ZIP create direct credential exposure.
entries.proves: Common plaintext env files and high-signal secret patterns are absent.
entries.doesNotProve: Secrets were not leaked elsewhere or deployed config is correct.
entries.failureHandling: Remove secret, rotate if real, regenerate safe examples/vault.

entries.productionRisk: Fake success, production mocks, or hidden placeholders can mislead owner/users.
entries.proves: High-risk fake-success and mock-production markers are absent from runtime/test paths and proof docs exist.
entries.doesNotProve: All future behavior is real; behavioral tests still required.
entries.failureHandling: Replace fake success with honest unavailable/failure state or explicit test-only lane.

entries.productionRisk: Founder promise/disclosure drift can mislead users or create legal/brand risk.
entries.proves: Canonical locked copy JSON contains required product and disclosure anchors; runtime loads JSON source.
entries.doesNotProve: Visual design or page placement quality.
entries.failureHandling: Update canonical JSON intentionally and keep runtime tied to it.

entries.productionRisk: Parallel TypeScript scaffold and plain .mjs runtime files can drift, causing validators to approve one path while the deployed app runs another.
entries.proves: Current app uses one canonical implementation path: plain .mjs runtime plus JSON contracts, with no TypeScript scaffold files under src/.
entries.doesNotProve: Browser visual quality, live providers, or future TypeScript build correctness.
entries.failureHandling: Remove scaffold drift or intentionally migrate to a real TypeScript build pipeline before continuing.

entries.productionRisk: App cannot produce deployable static output.
entries.proves: Current static build can generate route HTML/assets.
entries.doesNotProve: Deployed runtime, live functions, or visual approval.
entries.failureHandling: Fix build/runtime import/output error.

entries.command: node tests/domain/phase2-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase3-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase4-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase5-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase6-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase7-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase9a-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase9b-contracts.mjs && node tests/domain/phase9b1-contracts.mjs
entries.productionRisk: Core data/consent/AI/wisdom contracts can regress while routes still render.
entries.proves: Current phase domain contracts through Phase 9B.1 behave without provider theatre, numeric scoring, consent bypass, or brand/design parity regression.
entries.doesNotProve: Full browser Playwright journey, live providers, deployed runtime, or visual approval.
entries.failureHandling: Fix product/domain logic, not tests unless test is objectively wrong.

entries.productionRisk: Required founder-facing routes/disclosures may be missing or may claim unavailable features work.
entries.proves: Built static routes contain core brand/trust anchors and no fake completion claims.
entries.doesNotProve: Interactive journey, provider integrations, or visual polish.
entries.failureHandling: Fix route shell or disclosure anchors.

entries.productionRisk: Founder flow could capture/share/imply AI before consent/provider phases exist.
entries.proves: Question set, local card shape, inactive share state, and no fake submit/email/review claims.
entries.doesNotProve: Live AI, email, Network OS, or browser E2E.
entries.failureHandling: Fix local flow/card/claim boundary.

entries.productionRisk: AI provider path could leak secrets, fake output, skip schema validation, or lack honest unavailable state.
entries.proves: Server-side Gemini/OpenAI fallback contracts, schema/unavailable anchors, and env keys exist.
entries.doesNotProve: Live Gemini/OpenAI calls or deployed Cloudflare functions.
entries.failureHandling: Fix server provider/router/schema/failure state.

entries.productionRisk: Generic/fabricated/raw Scooter persona could enter runtime.
entries.proves: Approved wisdom is mandatory, raw sources blocked, no wisdom env mode exists, prompt includes approved context.
entries.doesNotProve: Scooter has approved future interview material or live AI quality.
entries.failureHandling: Fix approved-wisdom source/validator/prompt contract.

entries.productionRisk: Voice/avatar provider integration can leak secrets, fake media success, skip cost guards, or break static fallback.
entries.proves: ElevenLabs-first voice + talking-photo provider posture, fallback providers, request-level cost guards, safe unavailable states, and degraded static/text fallback contract exist for Phase 6/9D.
entries.doesNotProve: Live ElevenLabs credentials, paid provider account status, real media quality, persistent usage metering, headed gauntlet execution, or visual avatar approval.
entries.failureHandling: Fix provider contract, cost guard, fallback, env registry, or no-theater boundary before continuing.

entries.productionRisk: Critical current routes may fail to serve after build.
entries.proves: Built routes return 200 from local static server and include trust anchors.
entries.doesNotProve: Browser interactivity, visual screenshots, deployed hosting, or functions.
entries.failureHandling: Fix build/routes/server output.

entries.productionRisk: Visual/layout regressions can reduce trust, but container browser gaps should not block structural ZIP delivery.
entries.proves: Screenshots can be produced when Chromium is available.
entries.doesNotProve: Human visual approval or product correctness.
entries.failureHandling: Attempt and disclose. Resolve before production visual approval/COMPLETE if screenshots are required.

entries.productionRisk: Owner cannot create local env deterministically.
entries.proves: Placeholders can be generated from registry.
entries.doesNotProve: Real keys exist or are valid.
entries.failureHandling: Fix registry/script. Hard fail before local live provider validation.

entries.productionRisk: Encrypted env persistence may not restore local env.
entries.proves: Vault restore path works when passphrase is available.
entries.doesNotProve: Provider credentials are valid.
entries.failureHandling: Fix vault/restore script. Hard fail before live runtime proof.

entries.productionRisk: Cloudflare variables may be missed at deploy.
entries.proves: Names-only deploy plan can be generated from registry for implemented phase scope.
entries.doesNotProve: Cloudflare values are applied or valid.
entries.failureHandling: Fix registry/scope/sync script before deployment.

entries.productionRisk: None directly; each child command carries its own severity.
entries.proves: Convenience command references current hard-fail set.
entries.doesNotProve: Anything beyond child commands.
entries.failureHandling: Fix child command or validate:all composition.

entries.productionRisk: Same as env:create-local; force alias may overwrite local placeholder file intentionally.
entries.proves: Forced placeholder regeneration can run when chosen by operator.
entries.doesNotProve: Real keys exist or are valid.
entries.failureHandling: Fix registry/script; use with care locally.

entries.productionRisk: Same as env:local:from-vault; force alias may overwrite local env intentionally.
entries.proves: Forced vault restore path can run when chosen by operator.
entries.doesNotProve: Provider credentials are valid.
entries.failureHandling: Fix vault/restore script; use with care locally.

entries.productionRisk: Consentless, unsigned, fake, or auto-contact Network OS submissions would violate Phase 7 trust boundary.
entries.proves: Pitch Lab builds deterministic consent-gated payloads, signs server-to-server requests, rejects disabled/missing config, and refuses auto-contact success.
entries.doesNotProve: Live Network OS persistence, Google Sheets, real shared secret, deployed Cloudflare routing, or browser Playwright journey.
entries.failureHandling: Fix payload contract, signing, consent, or honest failure behavior before packaging Phase 7.

entries.productionRisk: Founder journey can feel incomplete, expose scoring risk, or claim unconfirmed submission success after email was removed.
entries.proves: Pitch Lab has copy-card path, qualitative Story Strength Signals, no numeric/fundability score, no email-me-card resurfacing, clear what-happens-next copy, and confirmed-submission-only thank-you behavior.
entries.doesNotProve: Live provider quality, browser Playwright execution, deployed Network OS handoff, or Phase 9B design overhaul.
entries.failureHandling: Fix journey copy/runtime validators or remove the risky claim/feature before packaging.

entries.productionRisk: Pitch Lab can regress into generic UI, missing West Peek logo/brand assets, hidden consent copy, or hard-to-read story-card surfaces before founder-facing release.
entries.proves: West Peek logo assets, black/white/orange palette, mantra hierarchy, story-card readability anchors, and consent/trust copy are present in built routes.
entries.doesNotProve: Human visual approval, pixel-perfect parity with existing Join West Peek apps, or headed Playwright behavior; Phase 9B.1 adds source-family parity checks.
entries.failureHandling: Restore brand assets, palette/mantra anchors, readability, or consent/trust surfaces; do not weaken disclosure for design polish.

entries.productionRisk: Pitch Lab can look West Peek-colored but still drift away from the preferred Ventures/Live app family or use fabricated brand assets.
entries.proves: Provided West Peek logo assets, Ventures/Live inspiration anchors, sharper editorial black-white-orange styling, and no-theater boundaries are present.
entries.doesNotProve: Pixel-perfect live-site parity, human visual approval, headed browser behavior, or deployed rendering.
entries.failureHandling: Restore provided logo, parity anchors, editorial styling, or trust boundaries before packaging.

entries.productionRisk: Level 5 product can appear complete while the capstone E2E journey, media requirements, consent boundaries, and provider failure states are not even wired for proof.
entries.proves: Master Gauntlet docs, Playwright config, e2e spec, fixtures, official Scooter photo asset, ElevenLabs-first provider posture, and required media moments exist and are wired for local/headed execution.
entries.doesNotProve: Headed browser execution, real ElevenLabs provider success, Cloudflare deployment, or live Network OS persistence. Those require the gauntlet run and real env/provider inputs.
entries.failureHandling: Restore missing gauntlet files, official media assets, provider posture, or no-theater assertions before packaging.

entries.productionRisk: Live provider/browser tests can fail from env, network, provider quota, or missing runtime media assets and should produce a repair report rather than block structural iteration.
entries.proves: Runs the consolidated live gauntlet report across validation, env vault proof, live voice, media provider proof, post-deploy functions, journey, browser, camera, and media browser lanes.
entries.doesNotProve: Provider availability forever, Cloudflare dashboard configuration, or that cached/generated media will stay playable after provider URL expiry.
entries.failureHandling: Read tmp/live-gauntlet-report/summary.md and logs; fix real failures while preserving secret redaction and .env.local handling.

entries.productionRisk: Headed browser proof can be blocked by local device permissions, camera availability, or operator interaction requirements.
entries.proves: Runs the consolidated live gauntlet in headed mode for browser/device-visible proof.
entries.doesNotProve: Noninteractive CI compatibility or permanent camera permissions on every machine.
entries.failureHandling: Use the consolidated report logs and screenshots; treat device-permission failures separately from product code failures.

entries.productionRisk: Media proof depends on runtime cache policy and provider env; false hard-fails can block normal iteration when product fallback remains honest.
entries.proves: Manifest source assets, runtime generation/cache contract, voice/avatar provider status, and dry-run no-fake media behavior.
entries.doesNotProve: Live paid provider render or committed reusable media assets.
entries.failureHandling: Fix missing manifest/cache/provider contract fields or run live mode with provider env for live proof.

entries.productionRisk: Paid provider render proof can fail from quota, env, network, provider status, or unapproved runtime media assets.
entries.proves: Live voice/avatar provider request path plus runtime media cache contract without requiring committed static clip files.
entries.doesNotProve: That every future dynamic final-summary clip is approved or reusable across sessions.
entries.failureHandling: Inspect provider-safe proof report; fix env/provider wiring or cache contract, not by hardcoding provider result URLs.

entries.productionRisk: Integral user promises can disappear from Playwright or live gauntlet coverage while validators still pass green.
entries.proves: E2E data flow anchors exist from profile/answers/AI story card/share/media surfaces into explicit Playwright or provider-proof lanes, including live LLM browser proof.
entries.doesNotProve: Actual browser execution, live deployed provider success, Cloudflare env correctness, or human visual approval.
entries.failureHandling: Restore missing journey/data-flow proof, add explicit Playwright/provider lane, or mark the affected promise UNPROVEN before delivery.

entries.productionRisk: LLM route can fake readiness or silently fall back unless direct provider boundaries are proven.
entries.proves: Invalid LLM input rejects, missing provider env fails honestly, and live mode can prove schema-backed AI Scooter response without secret leaks.
entries.doesNotProve: Long-term provider quality, cost forecasting, all prompt variants, or future production secret rotation.
entries.failureHandling: Fix env/provider/config/browser path. Do not mark AI Scooter live response as proven while this fails.

entries.productionRisk: AI Scooter cannot be considered live until a real configured LLM provider returns a schema-backed response.
entries.proves: Live configured LLM provider returns schema-backed AI Scooter story card with no guarantee/score/secret leakage.
entries.doesNotProve: Long-term provider quality, cost forecasting, all prompt variants, or future production secret rotation.
entries.failureHandling: Fix env/provider/config/browser path. Do not mark AI Scooter live response as proven while this fails.

entries.command: PITCH_LAB_LIVE_LLM_E2E=true PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_WEBSERVER=true npx playwright test tests/e2e/llm-live-response.spec.mjs --project=desktop-chromium
entries.productionRisk: The live app can pass API checks while the browser never shows the AI response to the founder.
entries.proves: Deployed browser clicks Generate AI Pitch Story Card, calls the live route without route stubbing, renders AI Scooter response, and stores the card locally.
entries.doesNotProve: Long-term provider quality, cost forecasting, all prompt variants, or future production secret rotation.
entries.failureHandling: Fix env/provider/config/browser path. Do not mark AI Scooter live response as proven while this fails.

entries.productionRisk: Scattered validation/proof scripts can create operator confusion, missed proof lanes, and false completion claims.
entries.proves: Runs or classifies the canonical non-live all-tests orchestrator; produces a single PASS/WARN/FAIL/UNPROVEN report and separates Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3, and Tier 4 proof boundaries.
entries.doesNotProve: Does not guarantee live providers unless live mode and restored env succeed; does not prove human visual/media/business approval.
entries.failureHandling: Inspect tmp/test-operations/summary.md and the referenced per-step log; fix exact failing lane or accept clearly labeled unproven layer.
